diagrams.title: Structure vs Union Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Comparison showing how structure allocates separate memory for each member while union shares memory for all members
elements: Structure box with 3 separate sections: int (4 bytes), float (4 bytes), char (1 byte) totaling 12+ bytes
Union box with 1 single section showing all 3 members (int, float, char) overlapping in same 4-byte space
Arrow labels showing 'Same Address' for union members
diagrams.purpose: Understanding memory allocation difference between structure and union

diagrams.title: Union Byte Interpretation
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how the same 4-byte memory location can be interpreted as different data types using union
elements: Four interpretations shown: as Integer (value=16961), as Float (unusual value), as Char array[4], as 2 shorts
Little-endian byte labels: byte[0]=0x41, byte[1]=0x42, byte[2]=0x43, byte[3]=0x44
Arrows showing same memory being interpreted differently
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how union allows same memory to be viewed as different data types

diagrams.title: Tagged Union Structure
diagrams.description: Diagram of a tagged union implementation showing type indicator with active member tracking
elements: Structure box containing: enum type_field (labeled 'Type: INTEGER'), union data (labeled 'Value: 10'),Labels showing 'Active Member: i (int)'
Flow showing how type_field guides correct interpretation
Legend explaining the purpose of each component
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how to safely use unions with type indicators
